header: Test vectors for BLS signature verification using the
min-pk variant (public keys in G1, signatures in G2)

EdgeCase.description: The test vector tests an edge case input.

EmptyMessage.description: The message is empty. Valid BLS signatures support empty messages.

FieldBoundary.description: The x-coordinate is near the field modulus, exercising boundary arithmetic.
FieldBoundary.effect: Implementations with carry propagation bugs may mishandle values near the modulus.

IdentityPoint.description: The signature or public key is the identity (point at infinity).
IdentityPoint.effect: The identity point as a signature or key is always invalid per the BLS spec.

InvalidEncoding.description: The serialized point has an invalid encoding.
InvalidEncoding.effect: Accepting invalid encodings may enable various attacks.

InvalidFlags.description: The serialized point has valid coordinates but incorrect flag bits.
InvalidFlags.effect: Accepting points with wrong flags can bypass validation or produce incorrect points.

InvalidSignature.description: The signature is not a valid point or does not verify.

LargeMessage.description: The message is large. Ensures hash-to-curve handles arbitrary length input.

NotInSubgroup.description: The signature point is on the curve but not in the prime-order subgroup.
NotInSubgroup.effect: Accepting points not in the subgroup enables rogue-key and other attacks.

NotOnCurve.description: The signature point is not on the curve.
NotOnCurve.effect: Accepting points not on the curve can lead to forgery or subgroup attacks.

SignatureMalleability.description: A modified signature that is mathematically related to a valid one.
SignatureMalleability.effect: Signature malleability can break protocols that assume signature uniqueness.

TruncatedSignature.effect: Accepting truncated signatures likely means signatures can be forged.

Valid.description: The test vector contains a valid BLS signature.

WrongKey.description: The signature was computed with a different key.
WrongKey.effect: Accepting such signatures means authentication is broken.

WrongMessage.description: The signature was computed over a different message.
WrongMessage.effect: Accepting such signatures means message integrity is broken.
